title: AC1 surveillance droid
content:
{{Top|leg|canon=AC1-series surveillance droid}} {{DroidSeries |image=[[File:AC1-surveillance-droid-SuperESB.png]] |name=AC1 surveillance droid
"Spy-eye" |creator=[[Cybot Galactica/Legends|Cybot Galactica]] |manufacturer= |designer= |homeworld= |line= |model= |class=[[Security droid/Legends|Security droid]] |degree=[[Class five droid/Legends|Class five droid]] |cost= |length=0.7 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] |width=0.7 meters |height= |mass= |gender= |sensor=[[Color/Legends|Red]] |plating= |armament=Light [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]] (optional) |equipment=*[[Repulsorlift/Legends|Repulsorlift]] engine (3-meter flight ceiling) *180-degree [[Holocamera/Legends|holocam]] *[[Holo receiver]] *[[Holotransmitter/Legends|Holotransmitter]] (500-meter range) *Spotlight |affiliation=*[[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] *[[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]] |firstmade= |retired= }} {{Quote|I've got five spy-eyes on Skywalker, Blackhole–!|O-625|"[[Gambler's World]]"}} '''AC1 surveillance droids''', also called "'''Spy-eyes'''," '''Surveillance remote droids''', or '''security remotes''', were essentially mobile, semi-intelligent surveillance [[Holocamera/Legends|holocams]]. ==Characteristics== [[File:AC1-surveillance-droid-Rebel-Alliance-Sourcebook.png|thumb|left|150px|A diagram of an AC1 surveillance droid]] They were equipped with a [[Repulsorlift/Legends|repulsorlift]] engine, a visual-range [[Holocamera/Legends|holocam]] (on a 180-degree swivel mount) with simultaneous [[Holotransmitter/Legends|holotransmitter]], and a [[holo receiver]] unit. The model moved about as fast as a [[Human/Legends|Human]] and was very silent, able to move in zig-zag patterns so rapidly that most species would not be able to see them. Within a range of 500 meters, the holotransmitter could continuously feed live images to a [[Security officer/Legends|security officer's]] [[Holodisplay/Legends|holo display]], and within that range the security officer could remotely control the droid by joystick if needed. The droids were not very intelligent and lacked personality. They would record suspects and relay the visual recordings, along with locational information, to a central surveillance station, then hide until they received further instruction or were taken over by remote control. Some at Battle of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]] were equipped with a light [[Blaster/Legends|blaster]]. ==History==
[[File:Surveillanceremote.jpg|thumb|150px|A security remote on the Death Star I]]
They were deployed by the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Empire]] on the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star I]] and at the [[Battle of Hoth/Legends|Battle of Hoth]]; they were also used as advance scouts by the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance]]. While [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]] and [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] where being [[Ambush on Tirahnn|ambushed]] by a group of [[Catuman]] warriors on the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] [[Tirahnn/Legends|Tirahnn]], [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]] patched into one of the local security's "spy-eyes" to track them down in the [[Tirahnn (city)|gigantic city]]; which allowed [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]] and [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]] to find their friends on time to rescue them.[[Star Wars Annual (1977) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 1]] They were also deployed by the Imperial agent known as [[Cronal|Blackhole]]; he used a host of them to track Luke Skywalker in [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]] on [[Vorzyd V/Legends|Vorzyd V]] without Luke knowing it. They were eventually destroyed by agents of the [[Natala Vanden|Vorzydiak president]]. An AC1 was modified by [[Ree Shala]] and equipped with dual [[DL-44 heavy blaster pistol/Legends|fire-linked DL-44 blasters]] and reinforced armor plating. It was left at Shala's base to ambush anybody who might be investigating the [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]].{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)}} ==Appearances== {{Mediacat|imagecat=Images of AC1 surveillance droids}} *[[Empire 11|''Empire'' 11]] {{Mo}} *[[Star Wars Annual (1977) 1|''Star Wars Annual'' (1977) 1]] {{Reprint|Star Wars Annual (1977) 1}} {{Mo}} *''[[Rebel Force: Trapped]]'' *[[Black Ice (adventure)|''Black Ice'']] {{ID|SpyEye}} *"[[Gambler's World]]" {{Reprint|Gambler's World}} {{1st}} *''[[Super Star Wars: The Empire Strikes Back]]'' *{{JournalCite|10|Desperate Measures}} {{Mo}} {{ID|spy-eye}} *{{DarkStryder|adventure=Harm's Way|book=The Kathol Rift|reprint=yes}} *{{JournalCite|6|Relic (article)|reprint=1}} ==Sources== *''[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook]]'' {{ID|AC1 Surveillance Droid" and "AC1 "Spy-Eye" Surveillance Droid}} *''[[Death Star Technical Companion]]'' {{ID|Security Remote}} *[[Death Star Technical Companion (Second Edition)|''Death Star Technical Companion'', Second Edition]] {{ID|Security Remote}} *[[The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook (Second Edition)|''The Rebel Alliance Sourcebook'', Second Edition]] *''[[The Essential Guide to Droids]]'' *{{FactFile|41|ESP 5-6|Espionage Droids}} *''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'' ==Notes and references== {{Reflist}} {{Interlang |de=AC-1-Überwachungsdroide#legends |nl=AC1 Surveillance Droid }} [[Category:Cybot Galactica products]] [[Category:Droid models of the Alliance to Restore the Republic]] [[Category:Droid models of the Galactic Empire]] [[Category:Probe droid models]]
